verifyCommand: npm run check-types && npm run test && npm run benchmark
acceptanceCriteria: npm run test passes (vitest, all files)
npm run benchmark passes 14/14 with no regression (Layer 3 is opt-in, OFF by default)
All non-skipped features have passes: true
notes: Phase 4 = Layer 3 (multi-file LLM mend via findReferences blast-radius), PROVE-THEN-BUILD. Layers 0/1/2/4 already ship. T-4-2 is the gate: if per-file iteration already converges on the forcing fixture, record the finding and skip T-4-3/T-4-4 (see guardrails SIGN-106). The loop builds only the DETERMINISTIC half (blast-radius, prompt builder, multi-file apply, wiring) with the LLM MOCKED; real paid LLM validation is the manual T-4-7 (skipped) per SIGN-104.

features.title: Deterministic blast-radius computation via findReferences
acceptance_criteria: New src/blastRadius.ts exports computeBlastRadius(opts) — given workspaceRoot + surviving Diagnostic[], uses ts.LanguageService.findReferences()/getReferencesAtPosition to return, per error symbol, the set of { symbol, declarationFile, references: {file,line,col}[] } spanning the workspace
Reuses the shared DocumentRegistry (src/sharedTsHost.ts) — no new lib double-parse; loads the WORKSPACE typescript, no bundling (SIGN-102)
Pure + deterministic: no LLM, no disk writes
src/blastRadius.test.ts covers a multi-file symbol-rename case (correct cross-file reference set) and a zero-references case
check-types clean; npm run test passes; npm run benchmark 14/14
features.notes: Independently useful (could back a future --blast-radius diagnostic). typeContext.ts already caches a Program per workspace; follow that pattern. findReferences spans are workspace-relative.

features.title: Forcing-function fixture + deterministic non-convergence proof (the PROVE gate)
acceptance_criteria: fixtures/forcing-multifile-ripple/ exists (mustPass:false, report-only) modeling a cross-file ripple where the locally-obvious single-file fix necessarily introduces an error in another file (e.g. a mutual rename / signature change that greedy per-file fixing oscillates on)

A deterministic test (src/multiFileMend.test.ts or blastRadius.test.ts) simulates per-file LOCAL fixes via a mock single-file fixer and asserts the diagnostic-signature set does NOT monotonically reach zero (oscillates or regresses) — establishing that runMendLoop's per-file iteration cannot converge, i.e. Layer 3 is justified
IF instead the mock converges: the test documents convergence, plans/progress.md records 'Layer 3 NOT justified — deferred', and T-4-3 + T-4-4 are set skip:true per SIGN-106
No real LLM call (SIGN-104); check-types/test/benchmark green
features.notes: The honest gate for prove-then-build. STATUS.md notes prior synthetic ripple fixtures converged via iteration; the job is to find (or prove the absence of) a case that doesn't. Mutual-rename oscillation is the candidate forcing pattern.

features.title: Multi-file mend prompt builder (deterministic, LLM mocked)
acceptance_criteria: A builder (e.g. buildMultiFileMendPrompt in src/multiFileMend.ts) folds the T-4-1 blast-radius set into a single prompt: every affected file + all reference sites grouped by symbol, reusing typeContext where useful
Unit tests assert the prompt contains every blast-radius file and reference site for the T-4-2 forcing fixture (no real LLM)
check-types clean; npm run test passes; npm run benchmark 14/14
features.notes: Depends on T-4-1 (blast radius) and T-4-2 (forcing fixture). Gated by SIGN-106: skip if T-4-2 deferred Layer 3.

features.title: Layer 3 multiFileMend() + wiring (opt-in, OFF by default; LLM mocked in tests)
acceptance_criteria: src/multiFileMend.ts exports multiFileMend(opts) — ONE LLM call spanning N files via the blast radius; applies coalesced multi-file SEARCH/REPLACE by reusing applyEditBlocks across files
Wired as Layer 3 between Layer 2 and Layer 4 in runMendLoop/runFullStack via an opt-in flag (e.g. multiFile: true / enableLayer3), DEFAULT OFF; emits LayerEvent { layer: 3, ... }
runFullStack test with a mocked Layer-3 _callLLM (returning multi-file edit blocks) resolves fixtures/forcing-multifile-ripple to 0 errors
Regression: with Layer 3 disabled, behavior is byte-identical to pre-T-4-4 (assert in a test); npm run benchmark stays 14/14 (Layer 3 off by default)
check-types clean; npm run test passes
features.notes: Follows the §8 'add a new layer' contract: invalidate the in-process tsc cache (resetInProcessTscCache) before re-validation. Gated by SIGN-106: skip if T-4-2 deferred Layer 3.

features.title: Extract PRICING to src/pricing.ts (single source) — resolves index.ts:451 TODO
acceptance_criteria: src/pricing.ts is the single PRICING table + costUsd() source; src/index.ts and cli/run-stack.ts import it instead of duplicating the table
No duplicated PRICING literal remains in index.ts or cli/run-stack.ts
Existing cost-computation tests (runFullStack.test.ts) still pass unchanged
check-types clean; npm run test passes; npm run benchmark 14/14
features.notes: Clean dedup. The TODO is at src/index.ts:451; cli/run-stack.ts has the mirror copy. Pure refactor — no behavior change.

features.title: Refresh ARCHITECTURE.md layer model + ROADMAP Phase 4 (docs)
acceptance_criteria: ARCHITECTURE.md §2 layer model no longer says Layers 2-4 live in spectoship2/ 'not exported' — reflects shipped Layer 2 (v0.4.0) + Layer 4 (v0.5.0) and Layer 3's actual status
ARCHITECTURE.md §13 (Layer 3 design) flipped from 'designed' to 'shipped' with the forcing-fixture outcome and any measured numbers — OR 'deferred' with the T-4-2 finding if Layer 3 was not justified
ROADMAP.md Phase 4 row marked done with what shipped
check-types/test/benchmark green
features.notes: Docs-only; depends on T-4-4 (or T-4-2's defer outcome) landing. Mirrors the Phase 3c docs pass.

features.title: Paid end-to-end LLM validation of Layer 3 against the forcing fixture
features.skipReason: Manual paid step — needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, is nondeterministic, and must NOT gate the unattended loop (SIGN-104). Run `npm run benchmark:llm` (or a real-model runFullStack with multiFile:true) against fixtures/forcing-multifile-ripple manually once Layer 3 lands, then record the result and flip the fixture's mustPass:true if it resolves.
acceptance_criteria: Manual: a real LLM (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5) running Layer 3 resolves fixtures/forcing-multifile-ripple in ONE multi-file call
Manual: result recorded in plans/progress.md; fixture mustPass flipped to true if it passes
features.notes: Intentionally skipped so the loop completes deterministically. This is the real proof that the mocked Layer 3 corresponds to actual model behavior.
